value.found_quote: Set a completion condition with /goal and Claude keeps working across turns until the condition is met... The /goal command sets a completion condition and Claude keeps working toward it without you prompting each step. After each turn, a small fast model checks whether the condition holds. If not, Claude starts another turn instead of returning control to you. The goal clears automatically once the condition is met.
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes a feature called `/goal` in Claude Code that allows users to set a verifiable "done" condition (the completion condition), after which Claude autonomously loops across turns until the condition is met — confirmed by a separate evaluator model after each turn. This directly substantiates the claim of "Goal-directed autonomous iteration." The page presents the feature as generally available (with a version requirement of Claude Code v2.1.139 or later), indicating active status.
